installAll.cli:
claude plugin marketplace add 21-BreakinCode/cc-plugins && \
  claude plugin install session-learner@21-breakincode && \
  claude plugin install autoresearch@21-breakincode && \
  claude plugin install harness@21-breakincode && \
  claude plugin install remotion-maker@21-breakincode && \
  claude plugin install hh@21-breakincode && \
  claude plugin install code-reviewer@21-breakincode && \
  claude plugin install uiux-optimizer@21-breakincode
installAll.update:
claude plugin marketplace update 21-breakincode && \
  claude plugin update session-learner@21-breakincode && \
  claude plugin update autoresearch@21-breakincode && \
  claude plugin update harness@21-breakincode && \
  claude plugin update remotion-maker@21-breakincode && \
  claude plugin update hh@21-breakincode && \
  claude plugin update code-reviewer@21-breakincode && \
  claude plugin update uiux-optimizer@21-breakincode

plugins.name: session-learner
plugins.tagline: Turn a session into atomic Zettelkasten knowledge
plugins.summary: A wrap-up → pick-up → recommend reflection funnel: wrap-up surfaces session pitfalls and candidate take-away topics, pick-up turns chosen topics into atomic Zettelkasten cards grounded in the real case and up to 3 web sources, and recommend picks the single topic most worth keeping.
plugins.oneLiner: Turn a working session into atomic Zettelkasten knowledge via a wrap-up → pick-up → recommend reflection funnel

skills.name: pick-up
skills.description: Use after wrap-up to turn chosen topic numbers into atomic Zettelkasten cards grounded in the session case and up to 3 web sources.

skills.name: recommend
skills.description: Use after wrap-up when unsure which take-away topic matters most — picks exactly one topic to keep, with reasoning.

skills.name: wrap-up
skills.description: Use at the end of a working or debugging session to reflect on pitfalls worth noticing and list candidate take-away topics to keep.

config.name: CLAUDE_SESSION_LEARNER_ZK_PATH
config.description: Vault `Permanent/` dir used to ground `pick-up` card links and tags.

plugins.name: autoresearch
plugins.summary: Runs an edit → eval → keep/discard loop on any artifact — code, prompts, or docs — scoring each change with a shell command, an LLM judge, or both. Keeps what passes, reverts what doesn't, and shows progress on a live auto-refreshing dashboard.
plugins.oneLiner: Iterative improvement loop with eval-driven keep/discard and live HTML dashboard, inspired by karpathy/autoresearch

commands.name: /autoresearch:improve
commands.description: Iteratively improve any artifact using an edit-eval-keep/discard loop with live dashboard

skills.name: experiment-loop
skills.description: Core iteration logic for autoresearch: edit target, run eval, keep or discard, update dashboard, repeat.

plugins.name: harness
plugins.tagline: Build the feedback machinery around your agent
plugins.summary: Scores project health across six categories, then scaffolds the Tier-1 harness components a project is missing — feedback loops, evals, sensors, and context-mgmt advisories — and auto-fixes the top-ranked issue by delegating to autoresearch.
plugins.oneLiner: Dev-lifecycle harness builder. Scans for missing feedback loops, sensors, evals, and oversized agents, then scaffolds Tier-1 components into your project's .claude/. Depends on autoresearch.

commands.name: /harness:build
commands.description: Menu-driven scaffolder for harness components — feedback loop, eval loop, sensor, or context-mgmt advisory. Writes Tier-1 artifacts into your project's .claude/.

commands.name: /harness:check
commands.description: Scan project health across code quality, tests, runtime, architecture, scriptability, and harness completeness — produces a scored harness report with impact-ranked improvements

commands.name: /harness:improvement
commands.description: Execute improvement loop on the top-ranked harness issue — auto-generates eval from probes and spawns the autoresearch:experimenter agent

skills.name: harness-probes
skills.description: Guide for adding custom probes to the harness check system.

plugins.name: remotion-maker
plugins.tagline: Generate styled Remotion videos, end to end
plugins.summary: A full pipeline for Remotion (React) videos: define a consistent style, generate scenes from your content, source free media, review preview frames, and verify against the style before rendering to MP4.
plugins.oneLiner: Generate Remotion videos with consistent style, media sourcing, and multi-tier verification

commands.name: /remotion-maker:create
commands.description: Full video creation pipeline: style -> generate -> preview -> verify -> render

commands.name: /remotion-maker:define-style
commands.description: Create or manage video style definitions for consistent series look and feel

commands.name: /remotion-maker:find-media
commands.description: Search free resources for media assets (images, videos, audio) for video scenes

commands.name: /remotion-maker:verify
commands.description: Verify generated Remotion video against style definition (3-tier: static, frame sampling, Playwright)

skills.name: remotion-best-practices
skills.description: Best practices for Remotion - Video creation in React

plugins.name: hh
plugins.tagline: Cross-context handover docs, LifeOS as the source of truth
plugins.summary: Bridges your Obsidian LifeOS vault and each repo through a ./handover symlink, so handover documents survive context switches and stay visible to editors, grep, Obsidian, and Claude alike. Includes a daily vault-wide wrap-up state machine.
plugins.oneLiner: LifeOS-as-SSOT handover document management via folder symlinks. Subsumes /op:wrap-up-today.

commands.name: /hh:init-org
commands.description: Scaffold handover_handler__initiation.md for the current ORG in $LifeOS/01Project/$ORG/. One-time per ORG.

commands.name: /hh:init-service
commands.description: Per-repo setup. Adds the current repo to the service mapping table and creates the ./handover symlink into LifeOS.

commands.name: /hh:new
commands.description: Create a well-formed handover document under ./handover/ (= LifeOS via symlink) seeded from the current conversation context.

commands.name: /hh:wrap-up
commands.description: Vault-wide daily wrap-up. Discovers active handovers, batches user decisions, executes archives/updates/suspensions in parallel. Replaces /op:wrap-up-today.

config.name: CLAUDE_HH_OFFER_NEW_AFTER_WRAPUP
config.description: When `1`, the Stop hook offers `/hh:new` after a wrap-up session (requires a working `./handover` symlink).

config.name: HH_ARCHIVE_ROOT
config.description: Where `/hh:wrap-up` writes archived handovers. Quote the value — it contains `$HOME` and spaces.

plugins.name: code-reviewer
plugins.summary: Layers a repo-specific review-mindset agent on top of pr-review-toolkit's 4+6 perspectives, citing your repo's own distilled principles, hotspots, and red-flags. Degrades gracefully to the standard review when no principle directory exists.
plugins.oneLiner: Principle-aware PR review. Layers a repo-specific review-mindset agent on top of pr-review-toolkit's 4+6 perspectives, with configurable principle-directory roots.

commands.name: /code-reviewer:review-pr
commands.description: Principle-aware PR review — layers a repo-specific review mindset on top of the standard 4+6 multi-agent review

config.name: CODE_REVIEWER_PRINCIPLE_DIR
config.description: Hard override; skip the resolution chain and use this principle directory.

config.name: CODE_REVIEWER_CONFIG_FILE
config.description: Override the config file that lists principle-directory roots.

plugins.name: uiux-optimizer
plugins.summary: Orchestrates live design references (refero.design + the getdesign.md catalogue), anti-slop taste discipline, and motion choreography across audit / build / explore modes and a gated ship pipeline. Degrades gracefully when the optional taste and motion skills aren't installed.
plugins.oneLiner: UI/UX design advisor — orchestrates reference-driven patterns (refero.design + awesome-design-md catalogue), anti-slop taste discipline, and motion choreography across audit/build/explore modes and a gated ship pipeline (direction → static → motion). Degrades gracefully when the optional taste-skill / motion-design-skill are not installed.

skills.name: uiux-optimizer
skills.description: UI/UX design advisor for improving visual design, layout, and component patterns AND for project-start design discovery.
